rt1-1-1.title: Hands-on: survey the invisible field around a source
rt1-1-1.intro: Roentgen's rays were invisible in 1895 and they are invisible now — only a survey meter shows you the field. Walk the rope boundary around a shielded Ir-192 exposure device, record the rate at all three stakes, and fix the one posting that reads over the limit.

tasks.label: Carry the meter to stakes A, B, C and record each
tasks.hint: Hold the meter still at a stake until the reading stabilises — Record only accepts a steady value.

tasks.label: Drag stake B outward until it reads the 2 mR/h limit
tasks.hint: The rate falls with the square of distance, so a short move outward drops the reading quickly.

rt1-1-2.title: Hands-on: inverse-square and halving math at the bay
rt1-1-2.intro: Put the lesson's math to work at a gamma bay: doubling distance divides the rate by 2 squared, each half-value layer is another negative power of 2, and the exposure plan is dose = rate x time rearranged for time. Solve all three with the live meter checking your arithmetic.

tasks.label: Double your distance — confirm the rate falls 4x
tasks.hint: I1·d1² = I2·d2²: doubling d makes d² four times larger, so the meter must read one quarter.

tasks.label: Stack HVL slabs until the door post reads under 2 mR/h
tasks.hint: Each half-value layer leaves 1/2 of the rate — after n slabs only 1/2^n remains, just like the halving table.

tasks.label: Plan 10+ minutes of work under the 20 mrem dose cap
tasks.hint: Rearrange dose = rate x hours: allowed hours = 20 mrem / rate. If that is too short, cut the rate with distance or slabs first.

rt1-1-4.title: Hands-on: watch a radioisotope decay on the clock
rt1-1-4.intro: Ir-192 — iridium with 115 neutrons instead of a stable mix — cannot settle, so its activity falls on a fixed 73.8-day half-life. Make a good exposure today, then jump 30 days ahead and compensate for the decay your radioisotope's nucleus made inevitable.

tasks.label: Expose the day-0 shot into the 2.0-3.0 density window
tasks.hint: Exposure E = activity x time — nudge the time slider until film density lands inside the window.

tasks.label: Jump 30 days and re-expose to make up for lost curies
tasks.hint: A = A0 x 2^(-30/73.8) ≈ 75% of day-0 activity, so stretch the exposure time by about a third.
